2606.15833.title: When Correct Edges Cannot Be Verified: A Provenance Gap in Incomplete KGQA and a Provenance-Favoring Completion Policy
2606.15833.paper_summary: Incomplete Knowledge Graph Question Answering (IKGQA) requires completing missing edges to continue reasoning. A growing line of work verifies completed edges against retrieved text, treating textual support as a proxy for edge quality. We ask a question that, to our knowledge, has not been systematically tested: does textual verifiability actually track correctness? Exploiting the gold deleted triples provided by the standard random-deletion protocol, we measure both. The finding is counterintuitive: among gold-correct completed edges, 76-96% have no supporting passage even under exhaustive retrieval, robustly across deletion rates (20%/40%), datasets (CWQ/WebQSP), and relation types (structural, commonsense, long-tail). Most Freebase-style facts simply do not occur as head-tail co-mentions in text. Textual faithfulness therefore measures provenance, not correctness -- separated by a paradigm-level gap no in-corpus retrieval closes. This reframes edge completion. Since most completed edges -- correct or not -- are causally redundant for the answer (95-97% of correct answers do not depend on any unsupported edge), the central question shifts from "is the edge correct?" to "admit or abstain under provenance uncertainty?" Within this framing we present TGComplete, a provenance-favoring admission policy that retrieves evidence at a reasoning breakpoint, verifies a candidate through a lightweight loop, and abstains when support is absent. Against the generate-to-complete baseline GoG, it attains higher edge precision against gold (15-21% vs 3-14%), with no statistically detectable EM loss and 3.1-7.4 times higher strict faithfulness of admitted edges -- at the cost of lower recall. We position TGComplete not as uniformly better, but as a principled point on a precision/provenance-recall trade-off, appropriate when auditability matters.
2606.15833.paper_summary_zh: 不完整知識圖譜問題回答（IKGQA）需要填補缺失的邊以繼續推理。越來越多的研究驗證了已完成邊的文本支持，將文本支持視為邊質量的代理。我們提出一個問題，據我們所知，這個問題尚未系統性地測試過：文本可驗證性是否實際上與正確性相關？利用標準隨機刪除協議提供的金標刪除三元組，我們同時測量了這兩者。這一發現是反直覺的：在金標正確的已完成邊中，即使在徹底檢索下，76-96%也沒有支持段落，這一結果在不同的刪除率（20%/40%）、數據集（CWQ/WebQSP）和關係類型（結構性、常識、長尾）中都穩健存在。大多數 Freebase 風格的事實在文本中根本不以頭尾共現的形式出現。因此，文本的忠實性測量的是來源，而不是正確性——這之間存在一個範式層級的差距，任何語料庫內的檢索都無法彌補。這重新框定了邊的完成。由於大多數已完成的邊——無論正確與否——對答案而言因果上是冗餘的（95-97% 的正確答案不依賴於任何不支持的邊），核心問題從“這個邊正確嗎？”轉變為“在來源不確定的情況下，接受還是放棄？”在這一框架下，我們提出了 TGComplete，一種偏向來源的接受政策，該政策在推理斷點檢索證據，通過輕量循環驗證候選者，並在缺乏支持時放棄。與生成以完成為基準的 GoG 相比，它在金標下達到了更高的邊精度（15-21% 對 3-14%），沒有統計上可檢測的 EM 損失，且已接受邊的嚴格忠實性高出 3.1-7.4 倍——代價是較低的召回率。我們將 TGComplete 定位為一個原則性點，而不是均勻更好，這是一個精度/來源-召回的權衡，適合在可審計性重要的情況下使用。
2606.15833.author: Yongqi Kang et.al.
2606.15833.authors: Yongqi Kang, Yu Fu, Yong Zhao
